hero_summary: Basta Nikar is a maqam of the Sikah family, built on Jins Sikah on Iraq (B half-flat) followed by Jins Saba on Dukah (D), and its personality appears through the balance between the Sikah tonic color and the Saba color in the upper region.
short_identity: A maqam of the Sikah family whose tonic is Iraq (B half-flat), and which in its concise Arabic form consists of Jins Sikah on Iraq followed by Jins Saba on Dukah (D).

quick_facts.character: A Sikah color at the tonic together with a clear Saba flavor in the upper region
quick_facts.common_start: It begins by stabilizing Iraq (B half-flat), then unfolds into Saba on Dukah before returning to the tonic

sayr.summary: Basta Nikar belongs to the Sikah family, and in concise Arabic explanation it has a clear structure: Jins Sikah on Iraq (B half-flat), then Jins Saba on Dukah (D). This is a correct structural formulation, provided that the first degree is understood functionally as Iraq (B half-flat), not merely as B. Its personality comes from moving between the Sikah color at the tonic and the Saba color in the upper region, and for that reason it is not well understood if reduced to an abstract scale alone.
resting_tones: Iraq (B half-flat): the tonic and final gravitational center of the maqam
Dukah (D): a decisive secondary resting point, because it is the place of the Saba jins that distinguishes the maqam
The Sikah color remains present below, but the special identity becomes clear when Saba on Dukah crystallizes in the upper region
motion_notes: This maqam is extremely sensitive in hearing; if Iraq (B half-flat) is not firmly established, its belonging to the Sikah family is lost
If Saba on Dukah does not become clear, it collapses into another maqam of the Sikah family
It should not be taught through a scale alone, but as a movement from a Sikah space on Iraq to Saba on Dukah, then back to the tonic

Inside the Saba jins there is an embedded Hijaz element on the third degree of Saba, so a detailed explanation may reveal an internal Hijaz presence without making Hijaz the principal jins of the maqam
common_path: Stabilize Iraq (B half-flat) as the tonic
Reveal the Sikah color at the tonic without blurring it
Enter convincingly into Saba on Dukah (D)
Complete the route without making it sound like a separate modulation into a new maqam
Return to the tonic while reaffirming the Sikah-family identity of the maqam
common_modulations: Iraq is also built on Iraq (B half-flat) inside the Sikah family, but it is not defined by Saba on Dukah as Basta Nikar is; the essential difference lies in the upper region and the sayr
Huzam belongs to the same family as well, but usually centers on Sikah (E half-flat) rather than Iraq (B half-flat), and its common melodic logic differs from the Sikah + Saba sequence of Basta Nikar
Saba is present inside Basta Nikar as the second jins, but the maqam as a whole is not Saba, because its tonic, family identity, and lower structural opening remain Sikah-based on Iraq
The sources checked here show no major disagreement over the basic structure: Sikah on Iraq plus Saba on Dukah

What differs is spread and practical handling: it is relatively well known in the Ottoman-Turkish repertoire and rarer in Egyptian and Levantine usage, while remaining strong as a modulation inside improvisation, especially in Huzam
Historical writing notes that what was once called Saba-Iraq later came to be known as Bastanikar
